reforms.description: The Austrian government adopted the Working Agreement on Research and Technology Policy (1994-98) to rationalise R&D support programmes, focusing on promoting business research spending and technology diffusion. This includes a new programme under the Innovation and Technology Fund (ITF) called Technologies for the Information Society, aimed at accelerating computerisation in both public and private sectors, and enhancing access to venture capital markets to foster the creation and diffusion of technological know-how.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance business research spending and technology diffusion, which are expected to raise productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The Working Agreement and associated programmes have been actively in force since their adoption.
reforms.importance_rationale: The adoption of a comprehensive working agreement and the establishment of new programmes represent significant structural changes in R&D support.
reforms.source_quote: The government adopted the Working Agreement on Research and Technology Policy (1994-98) which incorporates many recommendations of the OECD Technology/Productivity Job Creation Study.

reforms.description: Austria has rationalised its R&D support programmes, shifting the focus from traditional sectors to areas such as micro-electronics and information technologies through the Research Promotion Funds and the Innovation and Technology Fund (ITF).
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The shift in focus to high-tech sectors is expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The rationalisation of R&D support programmes has been fully implemented since 1997.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the allocation of public R&D funding towards emerging sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The emphasis is on the promotion of business research spending and technology diffusion rather than direct R&D support.

reforms.description: Austria's education and innovation system requires significant reform to enhance technological benefits and sustain a high wage economy, which includes reducing compartmentalisation in the education system and expanding the polytechnic system to improve knowledge transfer between academia and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving knowledge transfer is expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The need for reform has been identified and announced, but specific implementation details are pending.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed reforms represent a significant overhaul of the education system to better align with innovation needs.
reforms.source_quote: The education and innovation system needs changing to correct deficiencies and sustain a high wage economy.

reforms.description: The Austrian government has recognized the need to reduce barriers to business start-ups, including simplifying the legal and regulatory framework that currently delays the establishment of new enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing barriers to start-ups is expected to foster entrepreneurship and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recognition of the need for reform has been officially announced, but specific implementation details are pending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses significant structural barriers to entrepreneurship, representing a major policy shift.
reforms.source_quote: A great deal thus remains to be done in this area by reducing the number of registered trades and simplifying the requirements to establish a new firm.

reforms.description: The Austrian government aims to enhance the links between universities and industry to increase the transfer of knowledge between the academic and business sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening university-industry linkages is expected to facilitate innovation and improve economic outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative to enhance university-industry links has been actively implemented since 1997.
reforms.importance_rationale: While important, this reform is not as comprehensive as others and represents a significant but narrower change.
reforms.source_quote: Links between universities and industry should be developed, so as to increase the transfer of knowledge.

reforms.description: The Austrian government is addressing the challenges of limited access to venture capital and the need for stronger linkages between public and private research by enhancing skills development in industry and promoting technology diffusion, particularly among SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing skills development and access to venture capital is expected to foster innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.status_evidence: The recognition of the need for reform has been officially announced, but specific implementation details are pending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses critical barriers to innovation and entrepreneurship, representing a significant policy initiative.
reforms.source_quote: The government is addressing these problems by seeking to strengthen linkages between public and private research, and by enhancing skills development in industry.

reforms.description: A greater emphasis needs to be given to establishing industry-near innovation-oriented research institutes to enhance collaboration between academia and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Establishing research institutes close to industry is expected to facilitate collaboration and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The need for enhanced research infrastructure has been identified and announced, but specific implementation details are pending.
reforms.importance_rationale: While important, this reform is not as comprehensive as others and represents a significant but narrower change.
reforms.source_quote: A greater emphasis needs to be given to establishing industry-near innovation-oriented research institutes.

reforms.description: Venture capital markets remain underdeveloped in Austria, necessitating the removal of tax and regulatory barriers to enhance access to funding for innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Removing barriers to venture capital is expected to enhance funding access for innovative firms, fostering entrepreneurship.
reforms.status_evidence: The need for reform in venture capital markets has been officially announced, but specific implementation details are pending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant issue in the startup ecosystem but is not as comprehensive as major reforms.
reforms.source_quote: Venture capital markets remain underdeveloped in Austria and attention needs to be devoted to removing tax and regulatory barriers.
